name: Credit Union For Robertson County
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving Sumner, Robertson, Cheatham, Montgomery, and north Davidson County in Tennessee with checking, savings, loans, and financial wellness services.
description_long: The Credit Union for Robertson County (TCURC) is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union operating in Tennessee, primarily serving the Robertson County area and surrounding counties. The organization has been serving its community with a focus on accessible financial services and member support. TCURC offers a comprehensive range of traditional credit union products designed to meet the financial needs of individuals and businesses within their service area. Their service territory includes persons and businesses in Sumner, Robertson, Cheatham, Montgomery Counties, and the portion of Davidson County north of the Cumberland River, making it a regionally focused financial institution. The credit union distinguishes itself through active community involvement, including hosting annual Shred Day events (expanded to Shred Week) offering free document shredding for members and the community. They provide multiple account options, loan products, and member services tailored to their local membership base. The organization maintains both physical branch locations and digital banking capabilities through online banking enrollment and mobile access. However, the company recently experienced technical difficulties with online loan payment processing, requiring members to use alternative payment methods temporarily. Overall, TCURC represents a traditional credit union model focused on serving a specific regional community with personalized member service and locally-relevant financial products.
services: Checking accounts with online bill pay options (pay by check and card)
Savings accounts with published rates and fee structures
Personal and commercial loan products with online application
Online banking with login and enrollment for account management
Automatic bill pay and automated ACH transfers from external institutions
Auto Transfer enrollment for account-to-account recurring payments
Check reordering and EZ Card (debit card) management services
Member fraud protection and identity theft resources
Document shredding events (Shred Week) for community members
pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit credit union structure prioritizes member benefits over shareholder profits
Serves five-county Tennessee region with clear eligibility criteria for people who live, work, worship, or attend school in service areas
Offers both checking and savings accounts with published rates and fee schedules available on website
Multiple loan products with application available online and current promotions advertised
Free document shredding community service (Shred Week) demonstrates commitment to identity theft prevention
Direct member service support available via phone at (615)382-8255 with alternative payment options for loan payments
Financial wellness education and estate planning services available to members
cons: Recent online loan payment system outage caused service disruption, requiring members to use manual payment alternatives
Limited geographic service area restricted to five Tennessee counties, excluding most U.S. locations
Website does not display specific APR rates, fees, or product terms—members must contact institution or log in to view details
No evidence of mobile app or robust digital banking features compared to larger financial institutions
Membership eligibility requirements are restrictive and exclude those not meeting geographic or employment criteria
best_for: Residents and workers in Sumner, Robertson, Cheatham, Montgomery, or north Davidson County seeking community-focused banking
Local business owners and legal entities operating within the five-county Tennessee service area
Members prioritizing not-for-profit credit union structure and community reinvestment over corporate banking models
diagnosis: Best for residents and businesses within the five-county Tennessee service area (Sumner, Robertson, Cheatham, Montgomery, and north Davidson counties) seeking a member-owned credit union with personalized service and community involvement. Primary caveat: geographic service restriction limits accessibility to those outside the defined region, and lack of transparent rate/fee information online requires direct contact with the institution before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Credit Union for Robertson County (Springfield, TN) |.
meta_description: Credit Union for Robertson County — NCUA-insured credit union in Springfield, TN. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
